zh.Wortley, Yorkshire, England: 英國英格蘭越可爾沃特利
zh.Wortley, Yorkshire, England, United King: 英國英格蘭越可爾沃特利
zh.lifeSummary: 當Nicholas Wortley II於1400年出生在英國越可爾沃特利時，他的父親Nicholas Wortley I23歲，母親Elizabeth Waterton20歲。他於大約1431年與Isabel Tunstall在英國越可爾結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和6個女兒的父母。

zh-hans.Wortley, Yorkshire, England: 英国英格兰越可爾沃特利
zh-hans.Wortley, Yorkshire, England, United King: 英国英格兰越可爾沃特利
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Nicholas Wortley II于1400年出生在英国越可爾沃特利时，他的父亲Nicholas Wortley I23岁，母亲Elizabeth Waterton20岁。他于大约1431年与Isabel Tunstall在英国越可爾结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和6个女儿的父母。

nameMeanings.name: Wortley
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from Wortley in Tankersley (Yorkshire), or Lower, Upper, or New Wortley in Armley (Yorkshire), or (least likely) Wortley in Wotton under Edge (Gloucestershire). The place in Tankersley derives from Old English wyrt ‘plant, vegetable’ + lēah ‘wood, woodland clearing’. The place in Armley derives from an Old English personal name Wyrca or Weorca + Old English lēah. The Gloucestershire placename derives from Old English wyrt + lēah.

variants.name: Worley
meanings: habitational name with different origins. In the West Midlands this surname is most probably derived from Great or Little Wyrley in Norton Canes (Staffordshire). The placenames derive from Old English wīr ‘bog myrtle’ + lēah ‘wood, woodland clearing’. In the East Midlands, the name is possibly from Worlick in Ramsey (Huntingdonshire), the etymology of the which is uncertain. In northern England, possibly from Whirley in Over Alderley (Cheshire), which has the same etymology as the Staffordshire names. There may also be a further unlocated source of this habitational name in southern England.
possibly an irregular variant of   Wortley   or   Warley  .

nameMeanings.name: Nicholas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the post-classical Greek personal name Nikolaos, derived from nikē ‘victory’ + laos ‘people’. The spelling with -ch- first occurred as early as the 12th century, and became firmly established at the time of the Reformation, although Nicolas is still occasionally found. St Nicholas was a 4th-century bishop of Myra in Lycia, about whom virtually nothing factual is known, although a vast body of legend grew up around him, and he became the patron saint of Greece and of Russia, as well as of children, sailors, merchants, and pawnbrokers. His feast day is 6 December , and among the many roles which legend has assigned to him is that of bringer of Christmas presents, in the guise of ‘Santa Claus’ (an alteration of the Dutch form of his name, Sinterklaas).

variants.name: Nickolas
meanings: Mainly U.S.: variant spelling of   Nicholas  , influenced by the short form   Nick .

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Nicholas Wortley II was born in 1400, in Wortley, Yorkshire, England, his father, Nicholas Wortley I, was 23 and his mother, Elizabeth Waterton, was 20. He married Isabel Tunstall about 1431, in Yorkshire, England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 6 daughters.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Nicholas Wortley II est né en 1400, à Wortley, Everwyck, Angleterre, son père, Nicholas Wortley I, avait 23 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth Waterton, avait 20 ans. Il a épousé Isabel Tunstall environ 1431, à Everwyck, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 6 filles.

de.lifeSummary: Als Nicholas Wortley II 1400 in Wortley, Yorkshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Nicholas Wortley I 23 und seine Mutter Elizabeth Waterton 20. Er hat um 1431 in Yorkshire, England Isabel Tunstall geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 6 Töchter.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Nicholas Wortley II è nato il nel 1400 nel Wortley, Yorkshire, England, il suo padre, Nicholas Wortley I, aveva 23 anni e il suo madre, Elizabeth Waterton, aveva 20 anni. Lui sposato Isabel Tunstall circa 1431, nel Yorkshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 6 figlie.

ja.Wortley, Yorkshire, England, United King: イギリスイギリスヨークシュアイアウオルトリー
ja.lifeSummary: Nicholas Wortley IIが1400年にイギリスヨークシュアイアウオルトリーで生まれた時，父Nicholas Wortley Iは23歳，母Elizabeth Watertonは20歳であった。約1431年にイギリスヨークシュアイアでIsabel Tunstallと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と6人の娘の親であった。

ko.lifeSummary: Nicholas Wortley II(이)가 1400년 영국 요르크실르 워틀리에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Nicholas Wortley I(은)는 23세였고, 그의 어머니 Elizabeth Waterton(은)는 20세였다. 그는 대략 1431년 영국 요르크실르에서 Isabel Tunstall(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 여섯 명의 딸을 두었다.

mn.Wortley, Yorkshire, England, United King: Wortley, Eoferwic, Англи, Нэгдсэн Вант Улс

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Nicholas Wortley II nasceu em 1400, em Wortley, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Nicholas Wortley I, tinha 23 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth Waterton, tinha 20 anos. Ele casou-se com Isabel Tunstall aproximadamente 1431, em Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 6 filhas.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Nicholas Wortley II родился в 1400 г. в местности вортли, еркшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Nicholas Wortley I было 23 года, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Waterton, 20. Он женился на женщине по имени Isabel Tunstall около 1431 г. в местности еркшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 6 дочерей.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Nicholas Wortley II nació en 1400, en Wortley, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Nicholas Wortley I, tenía 23 años y su mamá, Elizabeth Waterton, tenía 20 años. Se casó con Isabel Tunstall aproximadamente 1431, en Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 6 hijas.
